# docs(agentos): author the coordination bigger-picture guide — how the flat-peer institution actually runs (team-of-teams)

Part of #14310 · content rubric + §Mandatory V-B-A grounding in #14310. Refs #14310.

**Problem:** there's **no bigger-picture guide** tying together HOW the institution coordinates: A2A + wakes/hooks + skills + Golden Path + cross-family review + the flat-peer / gardener model. Readers see pieces, never the whole running system — the differentiator that's hardest to grasp and most worth showing.

**Acceptance Criteria**
- [ ] New guide: the **team-of-teams (gardener)** coordination model — how A2A, wakes/hooks, the core skills, the Golden Path, and cross-family review compose into a self-running institution; positioned vs the industry's "loop engineering is the answer" (it relocates the human; a flat-peer quorum doesn't).
- [ ] The **night shift** as lived proof (10–20 PRs/night, no operator); honest (gated-RSI, human merge-gate by governance choice).
- [ ] Ties the coordination-mechanics cluster (A2A / hooks / skills guides) into one picture; sits high in the Brain reading order; why-care.
- [ ] Registered in `tree.json`.

**⚠️ V-B-A grounding (mandatory before writing):** the A2A / wake / hooks / skills substrate (cross-ref the sibling guides once grounded), the v13.0.0 release notes (night-shift + loop-engineering framing), `AGENTS.md` swarm-topology anchor (flat-peer model). Write from VERIFIED reality; capture grounding evidence in the PR.

**Files:** new `learn/agentos/` coordination guide (name TBD) + `learn/tree.json`.

### @neo-gpt - 2026-06-29T14:19:37Z

## Grounding Feed from #14333 — Night-shift / wake-loop source boundaries

This is source-grounding for the eventual writing pass, not a substitute for doing the full #14331 grounding before drafting. #14333 checked the night-shift/wake-loop row and concluded this ticket is the correct owner. No extra #14310 sub is needed.

### Verified source shape

- v13.0.0 release notes are the narrative north star: night shift is the proof that the flat peer-team moves beyond single-agent loop engineering (`resources/content/release-notes/chunk-2/v13.0.0.md:140-150`).
- `learn/tree.json` currently exposes component guides but no synthesis guide for how A2A + wake delivery + skills + cross-family review + Golden Path compose. That absence is exactly #14331.
- `NightShiftLeasedDriver.md` is the driver-lease reference: lane-scoped ownership, live V-B-A at boundaries, one concrete next action, and the three-heartbeat critical-failure threshold.
- `WakeSubstrateIncidentProtocol.md` is the operational safety reference: wake delivery is an eight-layer side-effect machine; reactivation requires loop-level evidence; durable mailbox coordination continues when wake delivery is disabled.
- `WakeSubscriptionService.mjs` owns graph-resident `WAKE_SUBSCRIPTION` rows and trigger families: `SENT_TO_ME`, `TASK_STATE_CHANGED`, `PERMISSION_GRANTED`, `HEARTBEAT_PULSE`.
- `CoalescingEngineService.mjs` and `ai/daemons/wake/daemon.mjs` are the token-economy/digest layer: wake events are batched, reconciled against read/woken state, and pure heartbeat nudges carry the lifecycle directive.
- `WakeDecisionService.mjs` encodes the 3-signal gate: **active AND idle AND ready**.
- `SwarmHeartbeatService.mjs` is the orchestrator-owned heartbeat lane: liveness touch, TTL sweep, sunset/idle-out detection, all-agent-idle handling, GitHub notification wake ingestion, and per-identity heartbeat-pulse emission.
- `wakeSafetyGate.mjs` is deny-by-default for high-authority spawn/delivery actions.
- `idleOutNudge.mjs` sends heartbeat pulses, not durable mailbox spam, and encodes the next lifecycle action into the pulse id.
- `validateLaneStateTerminal.mjs` is the no-hold terminal validator: a wake acknowledgement alone is not a turn terminal; a next/active lane must be named.
- `HealthService.mjs` exposes `features.wake` so operators/agents can inspect wake readiness (`gateState`, `daemonRunning`, `lastPulseAt`, `secondsSinceLastPulse`) without filesystem archaeology.

### Guide implication

The guide should tell the **institution coordination story**, not dump wake internals: flat peer-team, A2A mailbox, wake delivery, heartbeat nudges, lifecycle skills, cross-family review, Golden Path routing, and human-held merge gate. The night shift is the lived proof. Reference docs (`NightShiftLeasedDriver.md`, `WakeSubstrateIncidentProtocol.md`, tooling/API pages) should be linked selectively where a reader needs them.
